data.narrative:
AbCellera is a public-stage biotech and health company. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Tempus AI ($6.0B), Freenome ($2.6B), Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.8B), and Alto Neuroscience ($529M).

The peer-median method is applicable and yields $2.2B. This approach anchors to the median valuation across the four public biotech peers, which span a range of $529M to $6.0B. The public-comp anchoring method, which applies a sector price-to-sales multiple (7.8x median across 3 public tickers: RXRX, TEM, ONT.L), is not applicable because AbCellera's revenue is not on file. A complete triangulation would require revenue disclosure; the current analysis relies on one method only.

The quantitative analysis supports a range of $1.9B to $2.5B, with a midpoint of $2.2B. The width of this range reflects uncertainty inherent in relying solely on peer-median valuation without revenue-based cross-check. The peer sample itself exhibits material dispersion—the range from $529M to $6.0B—which introduces sensitivity to which comparables are deemed most similar. Resolution of this valuation range would require either AbCellera's disclosed revenue figures or additional operational metrics that correlate with valuation in the biotech sector.
